message:
feat(func): BuiltinFunctionFamily/Class taxonomy + surface inventory for Track-E parity accounting

Introduce the canonical taxonomy used by the Track-E parity closure
accounting (the built-in function parity matrix in fsqlite-harness)
so every parity tracker in the workspace uses the same labels and
the same authoritative list of built-in functions.

crates/fsqlite-func/src/lib.rs

- New std::sync::OnceLock import for the lazily-initialized
  surface inventory.

- New public enum BuiltinFunctionFamily { Scalar, Aggregate,
  Window } — the top-level "what kind of function is this from
  the runtime's perspective" classification.  Derives Copy,
  PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash so it works as both a
  map key and a sort key.
    label() -> &'static str → "scalar" | "aggregate" | "window"
    for structured logging / JSON emission.

- New public enum BuiltinFunctionClass {
      CoreScalar, MathScalar, DateTimeScalar,
      Aggregate, Window,
  } — the Track-E parity closure class.  Splits "Scalar" into
  three buckets (core / math / datetime) so parity accounting
  can report coverage for each family separately, while
  aggregates and window functions still map 1:1 to their own
  classes.
    label() -> &'static str → "core_scalar" | "math_scalar" |
        "datetime_scalar" | "aggregate" | "window".

- New public struct BuiltinFunctionSurfaceEntry {
      name: &'static str,
      family: BuiltinFunctionFamily,
      class: BuiltinFunctionClass,
      arity_signature: &'static str,
      track_e_bead: Option<&'static str>,
      availability_feature_gate: Option<&'static str>,
  } — one canonical row per built-in function.  name is the
  SQLite-visible function name; arity_signature is the normalized
  "(x,y,z)"/"(...)"/"(x[,y])" form used elsewhere in the codebase;
  track_e_bead is the owning Track-E bead id (e.g. "bd-trak-e.3")
  when one exists; availability_feature_gate names any Cargo
  feature that must be enabled to expose the function.

- New public fn builtin_function_surface_inventory() ->
  &'static [BuiltinFunctionSurfaceEntry] backed by a OnceLock.
  First call materializes the full list (~200 entries) and every
  subsequent call returns the cached slice.  The inventory is
  the single source of truth for:
    - the built-in function parity matrix in fsqlite-harness
      (replaces its previous hand-maintained list);
    - the parity score contract registration test;
    - the certification policy's Track-E gate inputs;
    - the documentation generator for the SKILL.md-style
      function reference in the canonical parity doc.

- The entries cover all of:
    * Core scalar: abs, changes, char, coalesce, format, hex,
      ifnull, iif, instr, last_insert_rowid, length, like,
      likelihood, likely, load_extension (gated), lower, ltrim,
      max (scalar 2+ arg), min (scalar 2+ arg), nullif, printf,
      quote, random, randomblob, replace, round, rtrim, soundex
      (gated), sqlite_compileoption_get, sqlite_compileoption_used,
      sqlite_offset, sqlite_source_id, sqlite_version, substr,
      substring, total_changes, trim, typeof, unhex, unicode,
      unlikely, upper, zeroblob.
    * Math scalar (gated on "math" feature per SQLite 3.35):
      acos, acosh, asin, asinh, atan, atan2, atanh, ceil,
      ceiling, cos, cosh, degrees, exp, floor, ln, log, log2,
      log10, mod, pi, pow, power, radians, sin, sinh, sqrt, tan,
      tanh, trunc.
    * DateTime scalar: date, time, datetime, julianday,
      strftime, unixepoch, timediff.
    * Aggregates: avg, count, group_concat, string_agg, max
      (1-arg), min (1-arg), sum, total.
    * Windows: row_number, rank, dense_rank, percent_rank,
      cume_dist, ntile, lag, lead, first_value, last_value,
      nth_value.
  (The complete list in the source is authoritative.)

- Unit tests:
    * builtin_function_surface_inventory is stable across calls
      (same pointer/len).
    * Every family has at least one entry.
    * Every class has at least one entry.
    * No duplicate (name, arity_signature) pairs.
    * Every non-None track_e_bead matches the "bd-*" shape.
    * Every window entry has BuiltinFunctionFamily::Window and
      BuiltinFunctionClass::Window and vice versa.
    * Every aggregate entry has family == Aggregate and
      class == Aggregate.

No runtime behavior change — nothing in this commit wires the
inventory into an active code path.  The harness-side consumers
that used to carry their own lists are migrated in the follow-on
harness commit ("canonical parity contract module + Track A
drift checks") already landed in this batch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
